threadTitle: Things that instantly reveal you just moved here
body: You still call it the Tube instead of just 'the Overground' when standing in the queues at London Fields station.
Last night on the 12 bus, a guy in a tuxedo was trying to impress a girl by quoting Shakespeare. He got every line wrong but she seemed charmed.

threadTitle: When you knew you were actually living here
body: When the bus driver waved to the lady with the shopping cart, and she shouted back about the streik, I knew I’d found my place.

threadTitle: Things Berlin does that nowhere else does
body: Only in Kreuzberg can you find spontaneous street parties where an old man plays accordion while kids dance around. It’s a slice of pure joy.

threadTitle: Real signs someone is from here vs just working here
body: Only a true local knows not to trust the 14-Mission schedule. You'll always wait longer than they say, especially at rush hour.

threadTitle: Muni moments that define this city
body: Yesterday, a dog chilled on the tracks at Civic Center while the whole station held its breath. Classic SF—both wild and oddly serene.

threadTitle: Things tourists get wrong about living here
body: Tourists think Gràcia is all about park Güell, but they miss the hidden murals and tiny plazas where locals actually hang out.

threadTitle: How to spot a recent arrival
body: They always look up at the street signs like they're in a treetop maze, trying to decode the Catalan names.

threadTitle: Things that instantly reveal you just moved here
body: You still think ‘The Lizard’ is a cocktail bar and not just a local pub with a dodgy jukebox.
Caught a whiff of fried fish on the 59 last night, turned out it was just a sleeping bloke’s takeaway spilling everywhere.

threadTitle: When you knew you were actually living here
body: It hit me when I saw a guy in a full suit bartering for a kebab at 2 AM, while street musicians played 'Wonderwall' in the background.

threadTitle: Things Berlin does that nowhere else does
body: You can catch a bus while people are rallying around it; the occasional protest just adds flair to the daily commute here.

threadTitle: Real signs someone is from here vs just working here
body: You can spot a local by their ability to navigate around the BART shutdowns. Tourists just stand there confused, staring at the platform.

threadTitle: Muni moments that define this city
body: Caught two teens arguing over the best taco spot while wedged between a guy snoring and a woman selling homemade crafts on the 14. Classic Muni.

threadTitle: Things tourists get wrong about living here
body: Tourists think the 'traditional' tapas bars serve the best, but the real gems are the unmarked places tucked away in the side streets.

threadTitle: How to spot a recent arrival
body: You can tell a recent arrival by their bewildered expression while navigating the packed Mercat de la Llibertat on a Saturday morning.
